dublinCore.dc:title: 119 HR 6999 IH: Tax Relief for Fraud Victims Act
dublinCore.dc:rights: Pursuant to Title 17 Section 105 of the United States Code, this file is not subject to copyright protection and is in the public domain.

action.action-desc: Mr. Miller of Ohio (for himself and Mr. Suozzi) introduced the following bill; which was referred to the Committee on Ways and Means

form.official-title: To amend the Internal Revenue Code of 1986 to repeal the limitation on deductions for personal casualty losses and to provide for increased taxpayer relief with respect to theft losses involving fraud, deceit, or misrepresentation.

section.text: This Act may be cited as the Tax Relief for Fraud Victims Act.

section.header: Repeal of limitation on deductions for personal casualty losses; increased taxpayer relief with respect to certain theft losses

subsection.header: Repeal of limitation on deductions for personal casualty losses
subsection.text: Section 165(h) of the Internal Revenue Code of 1986 is amended by striking paragraph (5).

subsection.header: Certain theft losses sustained during taxable year of choice; extension of period of limitation for credit or refund claims for certain theft losses

paragraph.header: Certain theft losses sustained during taxable year of choice
paragraph.text: Section 165(e) of such Code is amended to read as follows:
Except as provided in paragraph (2), any loss arising from theft shall be treated as sustained during the taxable year in which the taxpayer discovers such loss.

paragraph.header: Theft losses involving fraud, deceit, or misrepresentation
paragraph.text: In the case of any loss arising from theft involving fraud, deceit, or misrepresentation (as defined by the Secretary), the taxpayer may elect to treat such loss as sustained during the taxable year in which such loss occurs.

paragraph.header: Extension of period of limitation for credit or refund claims for certain theft losses
paragraph.text: Section 165(h)(4) of such Code is amended by adding at the end the following new subparagraph:

subparagraph.header: Period of limitation for credit or refund claims for theft losses involving fraud, deceit, or misrepresentation
subparagraph.text: In the case of a claim for credit or refund with respect to a deduction allowed under subsection (a) for any loss arising from theft involving fraud, deceit, or misrepresentation—

clause.text: the period of limitation prescribed by section 6511(a) for the filing of such claim shall be treated as not expiring earlier than the date that is 1 year after the date on which the taxpayer discovers such loss, and
section 6511(b)(2) shall not apply with respect to the filing of such claim.

subsection.header: Distributions relating to theft losses involving fraud, deceit, or misrepresentation
subsection.text: Section 72(t)(2) of such Code is amended by adding at the end the following new subparagraph:

subparagraph.header: Distributions relating to theft losses involving fraud, deceit, or misrepresentation

clause.text: Any distribution to the extent it relates to any loss arising from theft involving fraud, deceit, or misrepresentation for which a deduction is allowed under section 165(a).

Rules similar to the rules of subparagraph (H)(v) shall apply with respect to an individual who receives a distribution to which clause (i) applies, except that subparagraph (H)(v)(I) shall be applied by substituting 1-year period beginning on the day after the date on which the taxpayer discovers the loss described in subparagraph (O)(i) for 3-year period beginning on the day after the date on which such distribution was received.

clause.header: Period of limitation for credit or refund claims
clause.text: In the case of a claim for credit or refund of the tax imposed by paragraph (1) with respect to a distribution described in clause (i)—

subclause.text: the period of limitation prescribed by section 6511(a) for the filing of such claim shall be treated as not expiring earlier than the date that is 1 year after the date on which the taxpayer discovers the loss described in clause (i), and
section 6511(b)(2) shall not apply with respect to the filing of such claim.

subsection.text: Section 6511(i) of such Code is amended by adding at the end the following new paragraph:

paragraph.text: For a period of limitations for credit or refund in the case of theft losses involving fraud, deceit, or misrepresentation, see sections 72(t)(2)(O)(iii) and 165(h)(4)(F).
Except as provided in paragraph (2), the amendments made by this section shall apply to losses sustained in taxable years beginning after December 31, 2025.

paragraph.header: Distributions relating to theft losses involving fraud, deceit, or misrepresentation
paragraph.text: The amendment made by subsection (c) shall apply to distributions made after December 31, 2025.
